**Date and time:** Tuesday, March 17, 2026, 10:00–10:20 US Eastern / 7:00–7:20 US Pacific / 15:00–15:20 Central European

Information about the OQS project is available at [https://openquantumsafe.org](https://openquantumsafe.org). The project dashboard is at [https://openquantumsafe.org/dashboard.html](https://openquantumsafe.org/dashboard.html).

## liboqs 0.16.0 release planning

Douglas proposed targeting a liboqs 0.16.0 release, noting that it has been about five months since the last release (0.15.0, November 14, 2025). The plan is to spend the next two weeks wrapping up outstanding items and aim to cut a release candidate by the end of March.

### Items targeted for 0.16.0

**mlkem-native v1.1.0 update (PR [#2376](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2376)):**
Matthias submitted an update based on the new mlkem-native release. Douglas has reviewed it and invited others to take a look as well.

**sntrup761 code replacement (PR [#2356](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2356)):**
Douglas asked Basil to help resolve a conflict in the CBOM. Basil agreed to look at it after the call.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Basil):** Resolve the CBOM conflict in PR [#2356](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2356).

**HQC update (PR [#2328](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2328)):**
Douglas noted that Bruce will be working on the HQC update as one of his two main priorities over the next couple of weeks.

**MQOM (PR [#2361](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2361)):**
Douglas asked about including MQOM and other signature on-ramp algorithms in 0.16.0. Basil reported that SQIsign will likely not make it into this release and should target the next one. For MQOM, there is ongoing discussion in the PR about code size, as it adds a significant amount of new code. Basil explained that the copy-from-upstream mechanism supports common shared dependencies across implementations, which would help reduce the code footprint, but it does not yet work for MQOM's use case. Basil has created an issue and started work on extending this feature, and will submit a draft PR and communicate with the MQOM contributor about using it.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Basil):** Submit a draft PR extending the copy-from-upstream shared dependencies mechanism for use by MQOM and other algorithms.

**XMSS security report:**
Norm raised a recently reported security issue involving XMSS. The report initially described it as a buffer overflow, but Norm's analysis indicates it is actually a read past the boundary, with the out-of-bounds data being hashed as part of signature verification. Norm concluded it is a bug to fix but does not rise to the level of a security vulnerability, and sought agreement from the group. Hart noted in the chat that it is common for security reporters to overstate the severity of their findings. Douglas agreed the fix should be included in 0.16.0 if possible. Norm has a draft PR but is working through an unrelated build issue.

Douglas also noted that the project has been seeing an increase in security reports over the past month — more than one per week — likely due to increased attention to the code, possibly aided by AI tooling.

## OQS Technical Steering Committee

Discussion on the TSC chair role (issue [#246](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/246)) is ongoing, but no volunteers have come forward. Douglas encouraged people to consider the chair or co-chair role.

The PQCA mentorship program proposal deadline (originally March 13) has been extended, as many people were at or traveling to/from Real World Crypto last week. Hart confirmed that proposals are still being accepted.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (all):** [Consider submitting a project proposal for the PQCA mentorship program](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/240) — deadline has been extended past the original March 13 date.

## OQS-OpenSSL provider

A merge landed this week fixing up compatibility in preparation for OpenSSL 4 (PR [#758](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/758)). A few new issues were filed. Norm mentioned he has an outstanding PR he needs to work on and will try to get to it this week.

## OQS-OpenSSH

Two merges landed recently: PR [#190](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/openssh/pull/190) addressing a possible vulnerability in pre-authentication lengths for hybrid KEM, and PR [#189](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/openssh/pull/189) updating SPHINCS+ to SLH-DSA. No additional discussion.

## Next meeting

Next week's meeting (March 24) at 12:30 PM Eastern will be a **Technical Steering Committee meeting**. Douglas will prepare and circulate an agenda later this week. The next regular status meeting will be in two weeks.
